statement: The Mosco limsup/liminf pair identifies the closed lower-semicontinuous envelope of the augmented repaired core forms on the completed trace-fiber domain, and trace repair descent transports that closed envelope to the quotient space X_aug.

moscoLimsupInputStatus.label: Mosco limsup input
moscoLimsupInputStatus.reason: Recovery sequences give the correct upper envelope on the dense graph core.

moscoLiminfInputStatus.label: Mosco liminf input
moscoLiminfInputStatus.reason: Weak graph compactness and lower semicontinuity give the closed lower envelope.

traceRepairDescentInputStatus.label: trace repair descent input
traceRepairDescentInputStatus.reason: Finite trace repairs descend to a well-defined closed repair form on X_aug.

closedLowerEnvelopeIdentificationStatus.label: closed lower-envelope identification
closedLowerEnvelopeIdentificationStatus.reason: The Mosco limsup and liminf inequalities identify the closed lower-semicontinuous envelope of the core forms.

closedFormLowerSemicontinuityStatus.label: closed-form lower semicontinuity
closedFormLowerSemicontinuityStatus.reason: The Mosco envelope theorem identifies the closed form, and the trace repair descent theorem places the repair on X_aug with the transported quotient topology.

proof: Apply Mosco limsup to identify the correct closed envelope on recovery sequences.
Apply Mosco liminf to identify the lower envelope under weak graph limits.
Use trace repair descent to interpret the limiting trace form as D_aug on X_aug.
Conclude the transported repaired form is a closed lower-semicontinuous form on X_aug.
